title: Tracing the Sender
description:
A phishing email was reported by an employee. You have been given the email headers below.

Identify the originating IP address by locating the first 'Received: from' entry. Extract the last octet of that IP address.

--- EMAIL HEADERS ---

Return-Path: <spoofed@paypa1-support.com>
Received: from mail.paypa1-support.com (mail.paypa1-support.com [185.203.119.42])
        by mx.google.com with ESMTPS id k12si1234567qtc.123.2025.02.20.09.15.22
        for <employee@company.com>
        (version=TLS1_3 cipher=TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 bits=256/256);
        Thu, 20 Feb 2025 09:15:22 -0800 (PST)
Received: from unknown (HELO user-PC) (192.168.1.15)
        by mail.paypa1-support.com with SMTP;
        Thu, 20 Feb 2025 17:15:18 +0000
Message-ID: <20250220171518.12345@mail.paypa1-support.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Feb 2025 17:15:18 +0000
From: "PayPal Security" <security@paypa1-support.com>
To: employee@company.com
Subject: Urgent: Account Verification Required
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8

The flag format is FLAG{lastoctet}.
hints: Look for the first 'Received: from' line.
The IP address is usually in square brackets.
